flow.name: get:\employees:employee-details-api-config
flow.doc:name: Get Employees Flow
flow.doc:description: Retrieves all employees from the FTP data file. If the optional id query parameter is provided, returns the matching employee or 404 if not found.

logger.doc:name: Log GET Request
logger.doc:description: Logs the incoming GET /employees request with optional id filter

set-variable.doc:name: Store Employee ID Filter
set-variable.doc:description: Captures the optional id query parameter for use after FTP read

ftp:read.doc:name: FTP Read Employees File
ftp:read.doc:description: Reads the employees.json data file from the FTP server working directory

ee:transform.doc:name: Filter Employees by ID
ee:transform.doc:description: Returns a single employee when id filter is provided, or the full array when no filter is given. Defaults to empty array when FTP file is empty.

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json var employees = if (payload is Array) payload else [] --- if (vars.employeeId != null) (employees filter ($.id == vars.employeeId as Number))[0] else employees

choice.doc:name: Validate Employee Found
choice.doc:description: Raises APP:NOT_FOUND when a specific id was requested but no matching record exists

raise-error.doc:name: Raise 404 Not Found
raise-error.doc:description: Throws APP:NOT_FOUND when the requested employee id does not exist in the data source

logger.message: #['GET /employees - Response ready. Count: ' ++ (if (payload is Array) sizeOf(payload) as String else '1')]
logger.doc:name: Log GET Response
logger.doc:description: Logs the number of employees returned in the GET response

flow.name: post:\employees:application\json:employee-details-api-config
flow.doc:name: Create Employee Flow
flow.doc:description: Creates a new employee record. Validates no duplicate exists, appends to FTP file, and returns 201 Created confirmation.

set-variable.doc:name: Store New Employee Request
set-variable.doc:description: Saves the incoming POST request payload (new employee data) to a flow variable for later use

logger.message: #['POST /employees - Creating employee id=' ++ (vars.newEmployee.id as String default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log POST Request
logger.doc:description: Logs the incoming POST /employees request with the employee ID to be created

ftp:read.doc:name: FTP Read Existing Employees
ftp:read.doc:description: Reads the current list of employees from the FTP data file to check for duplicates before inserting

choice.doc:name: Check for Duplicate Employee
choice.doc:description: Raises APP:CONFLICT (409) if an employee with the same ID already exists in the data source

when.expression: #[(payload is Array) and ((payload filter ($.id == vars.newEmployee.id))[0] != null)]

raise-error.doc:name: Raise 409 Conflict
raise-error.doc:description: Throws APP:CONFLICT when an employee with the same id is found in the existing data

ee:transform.doc:name: Append New Employee to List
ee:transform.doc:description: Appends the new employee record to the existing employees array. Handles empty or null FTP response by defaulting to an empty array.

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json var existingEmployees = if (payload is Array) payload else [] --- existingEmployees ++ [{ id: vars.newEmployee.id, name: vars.newEmployee.name, salary: vars.newEmployee.salary, location: vars.newEmployee.location }]

ftp:write.doc:name: FTP Write Updated Employees
ftp:write.doc:description: Writes the updated employee list (with the new record appended) back to the FTP data file

set-payload.value: #[%dw 2.0 output application/json --- {id: vars.newEmployee.id, name: vars.newEmployee.name, status: 'Created'}]
set-payload.doc:name: Set Create Response Payload
set-payload.doc:description: Sets the 201 Created response payload with the new employee's id, name, and status=Created

logger.message: #['POST /employees - Employee created successfully. id=' ++ (vars.newEmployee.id as String default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log POST Success
logger.doc:description: Logs successful creation of the new employee record

flow.name: put:\employees:application\json:employee-details-api-config
flow.doc:name: Update Employee Flow
flow.doc:description: Updates an existing employee record by ID. Merges only the provided fields in-place — no duplicate keys. Returns 204 No Content on success.

set-variable.doc:name: Store Update Request
set-variable.doc:description: Saves the incoming PUT request payload (fields to update) to a flow variable for use after FTP read

logger.message: #['PUT /employees - Updating employee id=' ++ (vars.updateRequest.id as String default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log PUT Request
logger.doc:description: Logs the incoming PUT /employees request with the employee ID to be updated

ftp:read.doc:name: FTP Read Employees for Update
ftp:read.doc:description: Reads the current employee list from FTP to find and update the target employee record

ee:transform.doc:name: Update Matching Employee Record
ee:transform.doc:description: Finds the employee by id and overwrites only the provided fields (name, salary, location) — each field appears exactly once in the result.

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json var employeeList = if (payload is Array) payload else [] --- employeeList map (employee) -> if (employee.id == vars.updateRequest.id) { id: employee.id, name: vars.updateRequest.name default employee.name, salary: vars.updateRequest.salary default employee.salary, location: vars.updateRequest.location default employee.location } else employee

ftp:write.doc:name: FTP Write Updated Employees
ftp:write.doc:description: Writes the employee list with the updated record back to the FTP data file

set-variable.doc:name: Set HTTP Status 204
set-variable.doc:description: Sets the HTTP response status to 204 No Content as per the API specification for a successful update

set-payload.doc:name: Set Empty Response Body
set-payload.doc:description: Clears the response payload to comply with HTTP 204 No Content — no response body is returned

logger.message: #['PUT /employees - Employee updated successfully. id=' ++ (vars.updateRequest.id as String default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log PUT Success
logger.doc:description: Logs successful update of the employee record

flow.name: delete:\employees:employee-details-api-config
flow.doc:name: Delete Employee Flow
flow.doc:description: Deletes an employee record by the required id query parameter. Reads the FTP file, validates the employee exists, removes the matching record, writes the updated list back, and returns 204 No Content.

logger.doc:name: Log DELETE Request
logger.doc:description: Logs the incoming DELETE /employees request with the employee ID to be deleted

set-variable.doc:name: Store Employee ID
set-variable.doc:description: Captures the required id query parameter identifying the employee to delete

ftp:read.doc:name: FTP Read Employees File
ftp:read.doc:description: Reads the employees.json data file from the FTP server to locate and remove the target employee

choice.doc:name: Validate Employee Exists
choice.doc:description: Raises APP:NOT_FOUND when no employee with the given id exists in the data source

when.expression: #[not ((payload is Array) and ((payload filter ($.id == vars.employeeId as Number))[0] != null))]

raise-error.doc:name: Raise 404 Not Found
raise-error.doc:description: Throws APP:NOT_FOUND when no employee matching the provided id is found in the FTP data file

ee:transform.doc:name: Remove Employee from List
ee:transform.doc:description: Filters out the employee with the matching id from the array, producing the updated list without that record.

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json var employeeList = if (payload is Array) payload else [] --- employeeList filter ($.id != vars.employeeId as Number)

ftp:write.doc:name: FTP Write Updated Employees
ftp:write.doc:description: Writes the employee list with the deleted record removed back to the FTP data file

set-variable.doc:name: Set HTTP Status 204
set-variable.doc:description: Sets the HTTP response status to 204 No Content as per the API specification for a successful delete

set-payload.doc:name: Set Empty Response Body
set-payload.doc:description: Clears the response payload to comply with HTTP 204 No Content — no response body is returned

logger.doc:name: Log DELETE Success
logger.doc:description: Logs successful deletion of the employee record
